channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: High-spin transition metal atoms drive acidic oxygen evolution reactions
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69682-9

Developing non-precious-metal oxygen evolution reaction catalysts to replace precious metal components is a promising strategy to reduce the cost, yet it remains a challenge. Herein, the authors report a catalyst system with high-spin Co3 + , achieving efficient acidic water oxidation.
item.dc:title: High-spin transition metal atoms drive acidic oxygen evolution reactions
item.dc:creator: Xinyu Ping
Yurui Xue
Siyi Chen
Yunhao Zheng
Siao Chen
Yang Gao
Yuliang Li

item.title: Quantum coupling in colloidal homodimers of epitaxially attached CdSe@CdS dot@platelets probed on single-particle level
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69417-w

Coupled homodimers of quantum dots can provide a platform for investigating quantum phenomena. Here, the authors synthesize homodimers in solution exhibiting two orthogonally polarized photoluminescence modes with two distinct types of biexcitons.
item.dc:title: Quantum coupling in colloidal homodimers of epitaxially attached CdSe@CdS dot@platelets probed on single-particle level
item.dc:creator: Hairui Lei
Haiyan Qin
Haixin Lei
Yuqing Wang
Jiongzhao Li
Xiaogang Peng

item.title: An aqueous battery using an electrolyte with a pH of 7 and suitable for direct environmental discard
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69384-2

Aqueous batteries often rely on acidic or alkaline electrolytes that cause side reactions and environmental concerns. Here, the authors develop a covalent organic polymer negative electrode that enables long-life Mg²⁺/Ca²⁺ storage in neutral, environmentally benign electrolytes.
item.dc:title: An aqueous battery using an electrolyte with a pH of 7 and suitable for direct environmental discard
item.dc:creator: Hui Chen
Shuo Feng
Yutong Wang
Leheng Zhong
Wei Liang
Kangning Shi
Dedi Li
Jiaxiong Zhu
Huiling Cui
Zijie Tang
Haofan Zhang
Ran Li
Chunyi Zhi
Haiming Lv Lyu
